notes.note: The board of ten commissioners (decem legati) sent by the Senate to organize Greece after the sack of Corinth in 146 BC; Cicero is having Atticus research its membership for an antiquarian point in his current writing.

notes.anchor: and I think the same about Tuditanus
notes.note: C. Sempronius Tuditanus, consul 129 BC; Cicero dates his career relative to L. Mummius's consulship (146 BC), noting the son was quaestor a year after Mummius held office.
L. Mummius (Achaicus), consul 146 BC and the destroyer of Corinth; not to be confused with his brother Sp. Mummius, the legate (see 13.5).

notes.anchor: you will settle it with Piso
notes.note: A Piso acting as intermediary in settling one of Cicero's debts; he cannot be securely matched to any registered Piso and is left untagged. Not the Piso who speaks in the De Finibus.
The nomina (debt-claims) Cicero is taking over to fund the gardens purchase; he repeats here that he is content with them and presses Atticus to close the arrangement.
